steps.title: Find the key contradiction
steps.content: The passage establishes a key constraint: a basaltic surface would be unlikely to contain high silica. Yet Payré's data reveals exactly that—high silica detected on Mars. This incompatibility between expected composition (basalt alone) and observed composition (basalt plus silica) demands resolution.

steps.title: Determine the only possible conclusion
steps.content: Since the magma-ocean-cooling theory cannot explain the silica, additional processes must have formed the crust. D states this directly. A questions the reliability of silica data (contradicting Payré's use of it), B makes an irrelevant comparison to other planets, and C makes an unjustified leap to Earth's geology. That matches choice D — D is correct.
